mechanism_narrative: Monthly real retail sales (RSAFS) trending upward reflects robust consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month growth exhibits Rank IC < 0.02 with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or negative returns occur in >50% of months with positive RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7%, it signals significant labor market slack, which depresses consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 7%, or the average return after trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of very low federal funds rate and stable consumer price index indicates accommodative monetary policy without inflationary pressure, creating a favorable environment for equity valuations and positive 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Average 20-day return under trigger condition is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) or negative returns occur in more than 60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: During a manufacturing expansion regime where industrial production (INDPRO) is above its 12-month moving average and rising month-over-month, the index signals rising capacity utilization and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 12-month MA AND INDPRO month-over-month positive
failure_condition: Walk-forward 60-day returns in triggered periods are not significantly higher than non-triggered periods (Mann-Whitney U test p>0.10) or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth, measured by the quarterly chain-weighted index GDPC1, is the broadest measure of economic expansion; rising GDPC1 directly supports corporate earnings and equity prices over a 60-day forward horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate Rank IC with subsequent 60-day returns is less than 0.03 over any 10-year window, or negative returns follow positive GDP growth more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 900 thousand units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment, which historically precedes broader economic weakness and negative S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Average 5-day return after HOUST falls below 900 is not statistically negative (t-test p>0.10) or positive returns occur in more than 60% of events.

mechanism_narrative: When both nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) are rising simultaneously, it indicates a synchronized expansion in income and consumption, amplifying corporate earnings expectations and producing strong 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day returns in months with both PAYEMS and RSAFS growth are not significantly higher than months with mixed signals (t-test p>0.05) or the joint signal Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During a disinflation regime where the CPI index (CPIAUCSL) is declining month-over-month from previously elevated levels, the market expects monetary easing, which compresses discount rates and boosts equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day returns following a negative CPI month are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10) or are lower than returns following positive CPI months.
